hero.title: Hi, I'm Adrián Arroyo
hero.description: Fullstack developer specialized in Java, Spring Boot, and web technologies. Passionate about CI/CD automation, code quality, and scalable solutions.
about.title: About me
about.text: I'm Adrián, a computer engineer and fullstack developer with experience in university environments. I work with Java, Spring Boot, ZK Framework, and cloud technologies, building and maintaining web applications that serve the academic community. I'm passionate about process automation, continuous integration, and exploring how AI can improve development workflows.

footer.text: Designed and developed by Adrián Arroyo Pérez
project.1.title: Abstract Login
project.1.desc: Centralized and reusable authentication system for multiple web applications
project.1.detail.overview: Centralized authentication system designed to unify the different login flows that existed across multiple web applications in the organization. Previously, each application implemented its own authentication system, leading to inconsistencies and maintenance difficulties.
project.1.detail.tech: Built with Spring Security as the authentication and authorization core, ZK Framework for the user interface, and a dual authentication system against LDAP and a custom database. Integration with Azure Active Directory for silent sign-in, automatically detecting the user's email address.
project.1.detail.role: I designed and developed the complete system from scratch, including the authentication architecture, the abstract interface that visually integrates with each application, the roles and authorization system, and the Azure AD integration.
project.1.detail.challenges: The main challenge was unifying all existing authentication flows across different applications without breaking compatibility. Implementing an abstract interface that integrated with each application's visual components without causing aesthetic changes was especially complex. Configuring Spring Security to support multiple authentication sources (LDAP + custom DB + Azure AD) and Azure silent sign-in required a flexible architecture.
project.1.detail.results: The system is currently used by 5 web applications. Maintenance effort was significantly reduced by centralizing authentication, and user experience improved by offering a simpler and more consistent login process.
project.2.title: CI/CD Pipelines
project.2.desc: Continuous integration and deployment automation in Azure DevOps
project.2.detail.overview: Complete continuous integration and deployment infrastructure designed from scratch in Azure DevOps to automate processes that were previously done manually: building, deployment, quality analysis, and monitoring.
project.2.detail.tech: Azure DevOps as the main platform with YAML pipelines. Automated deployment to Tomcat and GlassFish servers. SonarQube for static code analysis and pull request rules. OWASP for security vulnerability detection. Kibana integration for log monitoring. AI (Claude) for intelligent report generation. Spring Cloud Config for centralized configuration management.
project.2.detail.role: I designed the entire CI/CD infrastructure from scratch in Azure DevOps. I configured multiple environments (dev on local machine, UAT with Linux agent, production) and established automated workflows.
project.2.detail.challenges: Parallelizing deployments across multiple GlassFish and Tomcat servers was a significant challenge, as each server previously required independent manual deployment. Integrating AI into pipelines to generate daily error reports from Kibana required designing effective prompts and connecting heterogeneous services. Establishing SonarQube rules in pull requests changed the team's work methodology.
project.2.detail.results: Deployment across multiple servers was parallelized with a single pipeline execution. AI-generated daily reports from Kibana enable rapid error detection. Weekly OWASP scans proactively detect security vulnerabilities. SonarQube rules in pull requests transformed the team's work methodology and code quality.
project.3.title: E2E Testing
project.3.desc: End-to-end automated testing framework for university enrollment
project.3.detail.overview: End-to-end automated testing framework for the university enrollment application, developed as a Bachelor's Thesis (TFG). The main problem was that every functional change in the application required manually repeating many tests with each student group to verify correct functionality.
project.3.detail.tech: Playwright integrated in a Spring Boot environment for test execution. Dynamic data retrieval from REST services. Report generation in Excel, automatic screenshots on errors, HTML accessibility reports, and execution statistics. Integrated with Azure DevOps CI/CD.
project.3.detail.role: Complete design and development as a Bachelor's Thesis project, supervised by María Luque Rodríguez and Antonio Arauzo Azofra at the University of Córdoba.
project.3.detail.challenges: Automating complex enrollment flows with multiple student profiles (new enrollment, master's, DECA, outgoing, renewal, bachelor's thesis, master's thesis) required modeling very diverse scenarios. Integrating user reset procedures and dynamic data retrieval from REST services added complexity. Accessibility, usability, performance, and payment gateway verification tests were implemented.
project.3.detail.results: Complete framework that automates enrollment testing for all student groups, eliminating the need for repetitive manual testing. Integrated into the Azure DevOps CI/CD pipeline for continuous execution. Generates automatic evidence with screenshots and detailed reports.
project.4.title: Online Platform Redesign
project.4.desc: Complete redesign of an online educational platform
project.4.detail.overview: Complete redesign of the university's online educational platform, which had a very outdated design based on an obsolete version of Bootstrap. The goal was to modernize the interface and meet web accessibility standards.
project.4.detail.tech: Java and XHTML for frontend development. Migration to the latest version of Bootstrap. Implementation of web accessibility patterns following WCAG recommendations.
project.4.detail.role: I performed the complete redesign individually, including the Bootstrap migration, accessibility implementation, and incompatibility resolution.
project.4.detail.challenges: Incompatibility between some components from the old and new Bootstrap versions required manual adaptations. Ensuring the entire platform met accessibility standards involved reviewing and modifying every interface component.
project.4.detail.results: The platform achieved a 100% accessibility score measured with Google Lighthouse. The user interface was completely modernized with an updated and responsive design.
project.5.title: ERP Improvements
project.5.desc: Functional improvements in enterprise resource planning system
project.5.detail.overview: Development of various functional improvements in the academic ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) system, responding to specific requests from university departments.
project.5.detail.tech: Java for business logic and ZK Framework for the user interface.
project.5.detail.role: I developed the functionalities individually, from requirements analysis to implementation.
project.5.detail.challenges: Understanding the specific needs of each department and translating them into technical features within the existing ERP architecture.
project.5.detail.results: Academic improvements requested by departments were implemented, enhancing the university's administrative workflows.
project.6.title: BIRT Reports
project.6.detail.overview: Editing and maintenance of enterprise academic reports using BIRT (Business Intelligence and Reporting Tools).
project.6.detail.tech: BIRT for report design and SQL for data queries.
project.6.detail.role: I edited and maintained existing academic reports.
project.6.detail.challenges: Getting familiar with the BIRT tool and adapting the SQL queries needed for academic reports.
project.6.detail.results: Updated and functional academic reports for university departments.
project.7.title: Cloud Config
project.7.detail.overview: Centralized configuration server that manages properties and configuration keys for multiple applications from a single point, eliminating the need for distributed configuration.
project.7.detail.tech: Spring Cloud Config Server for configuration centralization.
project.7.detail.role: Implementation and setup of the centralized server.
project.7.detail.challenges: Integrating the configuration server with multiple existing applications while ensuring compatibility.
project.7.detail.results: Most applications in the environment load their configuration from this centralized server, simplifying property and key management.
project.8.title: AI-Driven DevOps Automation
project.8.desc: AI-powered skills and automation for DevOps workflows
project.8.detail.overview: Development of an AI-powered DevOps automation ecosystem, including custom skills, MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers, Claude marketplace tools, and intelligent reports.
project.8.detail.tech: Claude AI as the artificial intelligence engine. MCP servers (local and Microsoft). Custom skills for automation. Integration with Azure DevOps pipelines. Automated OWASP and Kibana reports with AI analysis.
project.8.detail.role: I designed and implemented the entire automation ecosystem, including MCP configuration, skill development, and pipeline integration.
project.8.detail.challenges: Configuring certain MCPs, especially local and Microsoft ones, was particularly tedious. Setting up an agent on the machine for pipelines and connecting AI via command line without API usage represented a significant challenge.
project.8.detail.results: Daily OWASP reports with AI-generated solution proposals. Kibana reports with intelligent recommendations. Skills and tools published on the Claude marketplace. DevOps workflows automated with AI assistance.
project.9.title: Jakarta EE Migration
project.9.desc: Technology migration from javax to Jakarta
project.9.detail.overview: Technology migration from the javax namespace to the new Jakarta EE standard in existing university environment applications, as part of infrastructure modernization.
project.9.detail.tech: Jakarta EE as the migration target. Java for the migrated applications. Starter and dependency updates.
project.9.detail.role: I performed the application migration, including project and starter adaptation.
project.9.detail.challenges: Adapting projects to the new Jakarta structure and migrating starters presented technical challenges beyond simple package renaming, requiring configuration and dependency adjustments.
project.9.detail.results: Applications successfully migrated to the Jakarta EE standard, preparing them for future Java ecosystem updates.
project.10.title: PetWatch
project.10.desc: Pokémon-themed v-pet game for WearOS and mobile
project.10.detail.overview: Personal development project of a WearOS and mobile application that recreates a virtual pet (v-pet) game with a Pokémon theme. Includes an original combat system, quests, and habitats for the pets.
project.10.detail.tech: Kotlin as the development language. WearOS for the smartwatch experience. Companion app for mobile device. Turn-based combat system, quests, and habitat management.
project.10.detail.role: Personal project entirely designed and developed by me.
project.10.detail.challenges: Designing an original combat system inspired by Pokémon mechanics. Synchronizing the experience between WearOS and the mobile app. Creating a quest and habitat system that offers a complete gaming experience.
project.10.detail.results: Application in a functional and advanced state with combat system, quests, and habitats implemented. Pending improvements including new quests, polishing existing ones, and integrating new generations.
